name: Native Goby
description: Small native gobies that inhabit mountain streams and rivers. They are part of the local food tradition in remote Cordilleran towns.

name: Common Carp
description: Introduced to the Philippines, this freshwater fish is now common in the rivers and lakes of the Cordillera region. Also known as Tëmaginting (Silver Carp).

name: Mudfish
description: A resilient freshwater species found in rice fields and swamps. Valued for its ability to survive in low-oxygen environments.

name: Freshwater Shrimp
description: These river prawns are common in clean, flowing waters. They are often caught by hand or with bamboo traps.

name: Eel
description: Freshwater eels that migrate between rivers and the sea. Considered a delicacy in some upland Cordillera towns.

name: Sea Catfish
description: A native catfish species that thrives in river systems of the Cordillera. Often cooked in local sour stews.

name: Freshwater Snail
description: While often considered a pest in rice fields, kuhol is also harvested as a food source in the region and cooked in coconut milk.
location: Rice fields and waterways in Kalinga and Ifugao

name: Climbing Perch
description: A hardy freshwater fish that can breathe air and survive out of water temporarily. Often caught in paddies and creeks.

name: Black Pacu
description: A South American species introduced to Philippine aquaculture. Known for its fast growth and adaptability to upland fishponds.
location: Aquaculture sites in Benguet and Ifugao

name: Tilapia
description: Tilapia is a freshwater fish commonly raised in ponds. It has a mild taste, firm flesh, and is a staple in Filipino households due to its affordability and versatility.

name: Silver Carp
description: One of the most widely farmed freshwater fish in the Philippines due to its rapid growth, resistance to harsh conditions, and ability to reproduce easily.
location: Dams and lakes in the highlands.

name: Bighead Carp
description: A large freshwater fish prized for its meat and size; often raised alongside other carps in aquaculture systems.

name: Mudfish
description: A hardy native fish that thrives in shallow, muddy waters; a common food source in rural areas and valued for its flavor and nutritional benefits.

name: Catfish
description: Known for its ability to survive in poor water conditions; popular in backyard fish farming due to its low maintenance and fast growth.

name: Rainbow Trout
description: A cold-water species introduced to the highlands of Benguet; valued both for its meat and as a sport fish for recreational fishing.
location: Mountain streams and hatcheries in Benguet.

name: Freshwater Eel
description: Long-bodied fish that migrate between freshwater and saltwater; harvested locally and often smoked or grilled for consumption.

name: Freshwater Goby
description: A small native goby that lives in both clear and murky waters; often caught by hand or with nets in rural areas.

name: Mozambique Tilapia
description: An earlier tilapia species introduced for aquaculture; hardy and tolerant of poor water conditions, though less preferred than Nile tilapia.

name: Snakehead
description: A carnivorous freshwater predator similar to the mudfish, known for being aggressive and thriving in quiet, shallow waters.
location: Freshwater habitats in Abra and Apayao

name: Philippine Freshwater Climbing Perch
description: A tough little fish that can breathe air and move short distances on land; often seen in rice paddies and shallow creeks.

name: Manila Goby
description: A native species that plays a role in small-scale inland fisheries; caught using traps or nets and often served fried or grilled.
location: Rivers and lowland lakes in Kalinga

name: Asian Swamp Eel
description: Slender eel-like fish commonly found in muddy rice fields and wetlands; can survive in low-oxygen environments and is a popular delicacy.

name: Giant Gourami
description: A large, slow-moving fish raised in ponds and private lakes; appreciated for its firm white meat and peaceful nature.

name: White Goby
description: Similar to other gobies, it is commonly used in local dishes like soups or stews; found in rivers and lakes.

name: Silver Perch
description: A small, silver-colored native fish with soft meat; once abundant but now declining due to overfishing and pollution.

name: Red Nile Tilapia
description: A red-colored variety of Nile tilapia bred for aquaculture; popular for its mild taste and attractive appearance.

name: Common Snakehead
description: A predatory freshwater fish valued for its role in local diets and traditional medicine; easily adapts to various water bodies.

name: Java Barb
description: An introduced fish used in community fish ponds; helps maintain ecological balance and is a source of protein for many communities.

name: Grass Carp
description: A herbivorous fish used in aquaculture for controlling aquatic vegetation; grows large and is also harvested for meat.

name: Tilapia Hornorum
description: A hybrid tilapia known for improved growth and resilience, often raised in fish cages and tanks.

name: Climbing Goby
description: A small fish known for its remarkable ability to climb waterfalls and rocks; usually found in fast-flowing mountain streams.

name: Pait
description: Tiny freshwater fish commonly found in rivers and creeks; sometimes used as bait or consumed dried or fried.

name: Glass Perchlet
description: A small, semi-transparent fish sometimes found near river mouths or brackish waters; rarely used for food but interesting for display.
location: River mouths and brackish zones in lower elevation areas.

name: Swordtail
description: An ornamental freshwater fish with a long tail extension; sometimes escapes into the wild and adapts to small streams.

name: Mosquitofish
description: A tiny fish used in mosquito control because it eats larvae; commonly introduced in ditches and rice fields.

name: Guppy
description: A colorful aquarium fish that also survives in the wild; helps control mosquito populations and is easy to breed.

name: Zebra Danio
description: A striped aquarium favorite also used in research; survives in cool water and is found in community tanks and highland aquariums.
location: Highland aquariums and community fish tanks.

name: Paradise Fish
description: A brightly colored, air-breathing fish that thrives in rice paddies and cool pools; known for its beauty and toughness.
